report_name: DragonForce Ransomware Claims Attack on HELIX INTERNATIONAL, Threatens Data Leak

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for a cyberattack on HELIX INTERNATIONAL. The group operates a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) and employs a double extortion strategy, threatening to leak exfiltrated data to pressure the victim into paying a ransom.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data leakage.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: DragonForce ransomware encrypts files on victim systems as part of its double extortion attack model.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The DragonForce group uses commands such as 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to delete volume shadow copies, preventing easy system restoration from local backups.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The DragonForce group uses legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone.exe and megacmd.exe to exfiltrate large volumes of stolen data to cloud storage providers.

malware.name: DragonForce Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the DragonForce group in their double extortion attack against HELIX INTERNATIONAL. It is used to encrypt systems and exfiltrate data, with encrypted files potentially having an extension like '.dragonforce'.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: Used by the DragonForce group to exfiltrate large volumes of data to cloud storage as part of their double extortion tactic.

tools.name: megacmd
tools.description: Used by the DragonForce group to exfiltrate large volumes of data to cloud storage services like Mega.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by the DragonForce group to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery, a common precursor to ransomware deployment.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: Used by ransomware groups like DragonForce to delete volume shadow copies via the command 'wmic.exe shadowcopy delete'.

identities.name: HELIX INTERNATIONAL
identities.description: A U.S.-based software and managed services provider (MSP) targeted by the DragonForce ransomware group in a double extortion attack.

infrastructure.name: DragonForce Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web data leak site used by the DragonForce group to publicly name their victims and threaten to publish exfiltrated data as part of their double extortion strategy.

indicators.name: DragonForce Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via Vssadmin
indicators.pattern:
title: DragonForce Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via Vssadmin
id: 2b8c1f9a-1e3d-4b7c-8a6f-9d2e5b1a4c8e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment used by groups like DragonForce.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/dragonforce-strikes-at-helix-international
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used by ransomware groups like DragonForce to delete volume shadow copies, inhibiting system recovery.

indicators.name: DragonForce Exfiltration Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: DragonForce Exfiltration Tool Execution
id: 5d9e8c3a-7b1f-4d6e-9a2c-8b1d4f7a2c5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone.exe and megacmd.exe, which are frequently used by ransomware groups like DragonForce for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/dragonforce-strikes-at-helix-international
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common data exfiltration tools used by the DragonForce ransomware group.

indicators.name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/dragonforce-strikes-at-helix-international
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of WMIC to delete volume shadow copies, a technique associated with ransomware groups like DragonForce.

relationships.source_name: DragonForce Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via Vssadmin
Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
